title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: nccsdata R package downloading, filtering, analyzing NCCS (National Center Charitable Statistics) nonprofit organization data. Published Urban Institute.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Check package (equivalent of build + test + lint) R CMD check .  # Run all tests Rscript -e 'devtools::test()'  # Run a single test file Rscript -e 'testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-<name>.R")'  # Regenerate documentation from roxygen2 comments Rscript -e 'devtools::document()'  # Build pkgdown documentation site Rscript -e 'pkgdown::build_site()'  # Install package locally R CMD INSTALL .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: BMF parquet files stored public S3 bucket : s3://nccsdata/geocoding/bmf/{YYYY_MM}/merged/bmf_{YYYY_MM}_geocoded.parquet arrow package reads directly via S3 URIs — authentication needed.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/nccs_read.R — Core function. Reads BMF parquet S3 optional predicate-pushdown filters (state, county, NTEE subsector, exempt org type). Supports column selection lazy Arrow queries. R/nccs_summary.R — Grouped count summaries collected data. R/nccs_catalog.R — Lists valid filter values (offline, network needed). R/nccs_dictionary.R — Returns BMF data dictionary tibble optional grep filtering. Also documents bmf_dictionary dataset.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: data/bmf_dictionary.rda — 97-row tibble column_name, description, type BMF columns. data-raw/bmf_data_dictionary.csv — Source CSV S3. data-raw/data_generation.R — Script regenerate bmf_dictionary.rda CSV.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/ — Function files (nccs_read.R, nccs_summary.R, nccs_catalog.R, nccs_dictionary.R) man/ — Generated roxygen2 tests/testthat/ — Tests function vignettes/getting-started.Rmd — Introductory vignette (eval=FALSE avoid S3 calls build) _pkgdown.yml — Site config function references hex/ — Branding assets .github/ — CI workflows

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Imports: arrow, dplyr, utils Suggests: knitr, rmarkdown, testthat

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tests use testthat edition 3. Network-dependent integration tests use skip_on_cran() skip_if_offline(). test-nccs_dictionary.R — Dictionary dataset structure filtering (offline) test-nccs_catalog.R — Valid filter values error handling (offline) test-nccs_summary.R — Count summaries, grouping, CSV output (offline) test-nccs_read.R — S3 path construction, input validation (offline), integration reads (network)

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: nccsdata package provides access nonprofit organization data National Center Charitable Statistics (NCCS). reads IRS Business Master File (BMF) data stored parquet files public S3 bucket, support efficient filtering state, county, NTEE subsector, exempt organization type. package requires API keys authentication — data publicly accessible.

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: querying data, can explore 97 available columns using nccs_dictionary():
code: library(nccsdata)  # See all available columns nccs_dictionary()  # Find geocoding-related columns nccs_dictionary("geo")  # Find NTEE classification columns nccs_dictionary("ntee")

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: Use nccs_catalog() see valid values filter querying:
code: # NTEE v2 subsector codes nccs_catalog("ntee_subsector") #> [1] "ART" "EDU" "ENV" "HEL" "HMS" "HOS" "IFA" "MMB" "PSB" "REL" "UNI" "UNU"  # State and territory codes nccs_catalog("state")  # Exempt organization types (e.g., 501(c)(3), 501(c)(4), etc.) nccs_catalog("exempt_org_type")

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: core function nccs_read(), reads BMF data S3 predicate-pushdown filtering efficient reads.

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
code: # All nonprofits in Pennsylvania pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA") nrow(pa)

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
code: # Nonprofits in specific Pennsylvania counties nepa <- nccs_read(   state = "PA",   county = c("Lackawanna County", "Luzerne County", "Wayne County") ) nrow(nepa)

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
code: # Arts organizations in Pennsylvania pa_arts <- nccs_read(state = "PA", ntee_subsector = "ART") nrow(pa_arts)

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: BMF parquet file contains 97 columns 400 MB. default, nccs_read() returns curated subset commonly needed columns. can customize :
code: # Specify exact columns (minimizes download size) pa_slim <- nccs_read(   state = "PA",   columns = c("ein", "org_name_display", "geo_county", "nteev2_subsector") ) names(pa_slim)  # Get all 97 columns (slower) pa_full <- nccs_read(state = "PA", columns = "all")

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: Set collect = FALSE get lazy Arrow query instead tibble. useful building custom dplyr chains collecting:
code: library(dplyr)  query <- nccs_read(state = "PA", collect = FALSE)  # Add custom filters and transformations result <- query |>   filter(geo_county == "Lackawanna County") |>   select(ein, org_name_display, nteev2_subsector) |>   collect()

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: nccs_summary() produces grouped count summaries:
code: pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA")  # Total count nccs_summary(pa)  # Count by county nccs_summary(pa, group_by = "geo_county")  # Count by county and NTEE subsector nccs_summary(pa, group_by = c("geo_county", "nteev2_subsector"))

title: Getting Started with nccsdata
text: Write summary results CSV:
code: pa <- nccs_read(   state = "PA",   county = c("Lackawanna County", "Luzerne County", "Wayne County") )  nccs_summary(   pa,   group_by = c("geo_county", "nteev2_subsector"),   output_csv = "nepa_nonprofit_counts.csv" )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Thiyaghessan Poongundranar. Maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lecy J, Poongundranar T, Institute U (2026). nccsdata: Access Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data. R package version 2.0.0, https://github.com/UrbanInstitute/nccsdata.

what: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: nccsdata provides tools download, filter, analyze nonprofit organization data National Center Charitable Statistics (NCCS). reads IRS Business Master File (BMF) data stored parquet files public S3 bucket, support predicate-pushdown filtering state, county, NTEE subsector, exempt organization type. Note: version 2.0.0, ground-rewrite package. v1 API (get_data(), preview_sample(), parse_ntee()) replaced. See migration section .

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("UrbanInstitute/nccsdata")

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: nccs_read() downloads BMF data S3 optional filters. Filtering happens Arrow level via predicate pushdown, matching rows read memory.
code: library(nccsdata)  # All Pennsylvania nonprofits (default columns) pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA")  # Arts nonprofits in New York ny_arts <- nccs_read(state = "NY", ntee_subsector = "ART")  # Select specific columns pa_slim <- nccs_read(   state = "PA",   columns = c("ein", "org_name_display", "geo_county", "income_amount") )  # Lazy query for custom dplyr pipelines query <- nccs_read(state = "PA", collect = FALSE) result <- query |>   dplyr::filter(geo_county == "Lackawanna County") |>   dplyr::collect()

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: nccs_summary() produces grouped count summaries collected data frame.
code: pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA")  # Total count nccs_summary(pa)  # Count by county nccs_summary(pa, group_by = "geo_county")  # Count by county and subsector, export to CSV nccs_summary(pa, group_by = c("geo_county", "nteev2_subsector"),              output_csv = "pa_counts.csv")

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: nccs_catalog() lists valid values nccs_read() filters without network calls.
code: nccs_catalog("state") nccs_catalog("ntee_subsector") nccs_catalog("exempt_org_type")

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: nccs_dictionary() returns tibble describing 97 BMF columns, optional pattern filtering.
code: # All columns nccs_dictionary()  # Find geocoding-related columns nccs_dictionary("geo")  # Find NTEE-related columns nccs_dictionary("ntee")

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: Key changes: Data source moved legacy Core/BMF CSVs geocoded BMF parquet files S3. Filtering now uses Arrow predicate pushdown instead downloading full files. Dependencies reduced 12 packages 3 (arrow, dplyr, utils).

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: Full documentation available https://urbaninstitute.github.io/nccsdata/.

title: Access and Analyze NCCS Nonprofit Data
text: Browse getting started vignette Open issue GitHub Contact maintainer tpoongundranar@urban.org

code: bmf_dictionary

text: tibble 97 rows 3 columns: column_name Column name BMF parquet file description Human-readable description column type Data type (character, integer, numeric, logical, IDate)

title: BMF Data Dictionary — bmf_dictionary
text: NCCS S3 bucket data dictionary

title: List Valid Filter Values — nccs_catalog
text: Returns set valid values filters used [nccs_read()]. network access needed.

title: List Valid Filter Values — nccs_catalog
code: nccs_catalog(field = c("ntee_subsector", "exempt_org_type", "state"))

title: List Valid Filter Values — nccs_catalog
text: field One `"ntee_subsector"`, `"exempt_org_type"`, `"state"`.

title: List Valid Filter Values — nccs_catalog
text: character vector valid values specified field.

what: Browse the BMF Data Dictionary — nccs_dictionary
title: Browse the BMF Data Dictionary — nccs_dictionary
text: Returns BMF data dictionary tibble, optional grep filtering column names. Use discover available columns calling [nccs_read()].

title: Browse the BMF Data Dictionary — nccs_dictionary
code: nccs_dictionary(pattern = NULL)

title: Browse the BMF Data Dictionary — nccs_dictionary
text: pattern Optional character string. provided, filters dictionary column names matching pattern (case-insensitive regular expression).

title: Browse the BMF Data Dictionary — nccs_dictionary
text: tibble columns `column_name`, `description`, `type`.

title: Browse the BMF Data Dictionary — nccs_dictionary

what: Read NCCS BMF Data from S3 — nccs_read
title: Read NCCS BMF Data from S3 — nccs_read
text: Reads NCCS Business Master File (BMF) stored parquet file public S3 bucket. Supports predicate-pushdown filtering state, county, NTEE subsector, exempt organization type efficient reads.

title: Read NCCS BMF Data from S3 — nccs_read
code: nccs_read(   state = NULL,   ntee_subsector = NULL,   exempt_org_type = NULL,   county = NULL,   columns = NULL,   date = "2026_03",   collect = TRUE )

title: Read NCCS BMF Data from S3 — nccs_read
text: state Character vector two-letter state abbreviations filter (e.g., `"PA"`, `c("PA", "NY")`). Filters `org_addr_state` column. ntee_subsector Character vector NTEE v2 subsector codes (e.g., `"ART"`, `c("ART", "EDU")`). See [nccs_catalog()] valid values. Filters `nteev2_subsector` column. exempt_org_type Character vector exempt organization type descriptions. See [nccs_catalog()] valid values. Filters `exempt_organization_type` column. county Character vector county names (e.g., `"Lackawanna County"`). Filters `geo_county` column. columns Column selection. `NULL` (default) returns sensible default subset. character vector returns specific columns. `""` returns columns (warning: 400+ MB). Columns used active filters always included. date Character string `YYYY_MM` format indicating data vintage. Defaults `"2026_03"`. collect Logical. `TRUE` (default), collects result tibble. `FALSE`, returns lazy Arrow query dplyr operations.

title: Read NCCS BMF Data from S3 — nccs_read
text: tibble (`collect = TRUE`) Arrow Dataset query (  `collect = FALSE`).

title: Read NCCS BMF Data from S3 — nccs_read
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Read all Pennsylvania nonprofits pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA")  # Read with specific columns pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA", columns = c("ein", "org_name_display", "geo_county"))  # Lazy query for custom dplyr chains query <- nccs_read(state = "PA", collect = FALSE) result <- query |> dplyr::filter(geo_county == "Lackawanna County") |> dplyr::collect() } # }

title: Grouped Count Summaries — nccs_summary
text: Produces grouped count summaries collected BMF data frame. Useful generating county-level subsector-level nonprofit counts.

title: Grouped Count Summaries — nccs_summary
code: nccs_summary(data, group_by = NULL, output_csv = NULL)

title: Grouped Count Summaries — nccs_summary
text: data data frame tibble, typically output [nccs_read()]. group_by Character vector column names group counting. `NULL` (default), returns total row count. output_csv Optional file path. provided, writes result CSV file.

title: Grouped Count Summaries — nccs_summary
text: tibble grouping columns () count column `n`.

title: Grouped Count Summaries — nccs_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pa <- nccs_read(state = "PA")  # Total count nccs_summary(pa)  # Count by county nccs_summary(pa, group_by = "geo_county")  # Count by county and subsector, save to CSV nccs_summary(pa, group_by = c("geo_county", "nteev2_subsector"),              output_csv = "counts.csv") } # }
